output:
Here's the clean document using GFM patterns:

---

# Project Migration Plan

## Overview

Migrate the application stack from Express + Postgres 14 on Heroku to Hono + Postgres 16 on Railway.

## Why Migrate?

| Component | Current | Target | Reason |
|:----------|:--------|:-------|:-------|
| API Framework | Express | Hono | ~3× faster request handling |
| Database | Postgres 14 | Postgres 16 | Improved JSON support |
| Hosting | Heroku | Railway | Lower cost |

## Priorities

| Task | Priority |
|:-----|:--------:|
| Express → Hono | 🔴 High |
| Postgres 14 → 16 | 🟡 Medium |
| Heroku → Railway | 🟢 Low |

## Task List

- [ ] Plan — define scope, assign owners, freeze feature work
- [ ] Migrate API — replace Express routes with Hono equivalents
- [ ] Migrate DB — upgrade Postgres 14 → 16, validate schema and data
- [ ] Test — integration and regression tests across all endpoints
- [ ] Deploy — cut over to Railway, monitor, decommission Heroku

## Migration Flow

```mermaid
graph LR
  A[Plan] --> B[Migrate API]
  B --> C[Migrate DB]
  C --> D[Test]
  D --> E[Deploy]
```

> [!NOTE]
> The API and DB migrations are sequential — Hono must be stable before the database upgrade begins to isolate variables during testing.

---

Renderer target: **GitHub Flavored Markdown**. The `> [!NOTE]` callout and Mermaid diagram both require GFM support (GitHub, GitLab 16+). For other renderers, replace the callout with a plain blockquote.

assertions.reasoning: The document includes a properly formatted Markdown comparison table with Component, Current, Target, and Reason columns covering all three migration components.

assertions.text: The document includes a task list using Markdown checkbox syntax (- [ ]).
assertions.reasoning: The document includes a task list with five items using Markdown checkbox syntax (- [ ]).
